{{Short description|Film award ceremony in 2022}} {{pp-sock|small=yes}} {{Use American English|date=December 2021}} {{Use mdy dates|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox film awards | number = 79 | award = Golden Globe Awards | date = January 9, 2022 | site = [[The Beverly Hilton]],
[[Beverly Hills, California]], U.S. | best_film_drama = ''[[The Power of the Dog (film)|The Power of the Dog]]'' | best_film_musical_or_comedy = ''[[West Side Story (2021 film)|West Side Story]]'' | best_drama_series = ''[[Succession (TV series)|Succession]]'' | best_musical_or_comedy_series = ''[[Hacks (TV series)|Hacks]]'' | best_miniseries_or_tv_movie = ''[[The Underground Railroad (miniseries)|The Underground Railroad]]'' | most_wins = ''[[The Power of the Dog (film)|The Power of the Dog]]''
''[[Succession (TV series)|Succession]]''
''[[West Side Story (2021 film)|West Side Story]]'' (3) | most_nominations = ''[[Belfast (film)|Belfast]]''
''[[The Power of the Dog (film)|The Power of the Dog]]'' (7) | last = [[78th Golden Globe Awards|78th]] | next = [[80th Golden Globe Awards|80th]] }} The '''79th Golden Globe Awards''' honored the best in [[2021 in film|film]] and [[2021 in American television|American television of 2021]], as chosen by the [[Hollywood Foreign Press Association]] (HFPA). The ceremony took place privately on January 9, 2022.{{cite web |last=Littleton |first=Cynthia |date=October 15, 2021 |title=Golden Globe Awards Set for Jan. 9 as Hollywood Foreign Press Assn. Unveils 2022 Calendar |url=https://variety.com/2021/film/news/golden-globe-awards-hfpa-awards-calendar-1235090499/ |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |access-date=October 16, 2021}} The nominees were announced on December 13, 2021, by rapper [[Snoop Dogg]] and HFPA president Helen Hoehne.{{cite web |url=https://www.indiewire.com/awards/industry/golden-globe-nominations-2022-see-full-list-1234685129/ |title=2022 Golden Globe Nominations: 'Licorice Pizza', 'Squid Game', 'West Side Story', and More |last=Lattanzio |first=Ray |date=December 13, 2021 |publisher=[[IndieWire]] |access-date=December 13, 2021}} For the first time since [[65th Golden Globe Awards|2008]], there was no traditional, televised ceremony. In support of [[boycott]]s of the HFPA by various media companies, actors, and other creatives over its lack of action to increase the membership [[Diversity (business)|diversity]] of the organization, the Golden Globes' regular broadcaster [[NBC]] declined to televise the 79th Golden Globe Awards. The HFPA ultimately chose to hold the presentation privately, with attendance limited to the organization's beneficiaries, and results announced via [[press release]] and highlighted on the Golden Globes' [[social media]] pages. ''[[Belfast (film)|Belfast]]'' and ''[[The Power of the Dog (film)|The Power of the Dog]]'' tied for the most nominations with seven each, while the latter's three awards (including [[Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Drama|Best Motion Picture – Drama]]) tied with ''[[West Side Story (2021 film)|West Side Story]]'' ([[Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy|Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy]]) and [[HBO]]'s ''[[Succession (TV series)|Succession]]'' ([[Golden Globe Award for Best Television Series – Drama|Best Television Series – Drama]]) for the most wins of the night. ==Ceremony information== {{See also|Hollywood Foreign Press Association#Black representation}} On May 10, 2021, [[NBC]] announced that it would not televise the ceremony, in support of a [[boycott]] of the HFPA by multiple media companies over what it criticized as insufficient efforts to increase the membership [[Diversity (business)|diversity]] of the organization. NBC added that it would be open to televising the ceremony in 2023 if the HFPA were successful in its efforts to reform its organization.{{cite web |last=Ausiello |first=Michael |date=May 10, 2021 |title=Golden Globes Cancelled: NBC Scraps 2022 Ceremony as Backlash Grows |url=https://tvline.com/2021/05/10/golden-globes-cancelled-nbc-2022-ceremony-controversy/ |work=[[TVLine]] |access-date=October 16, 2021}} Following these events, the HFPA released a timeline for its reforms, which would see the process completed by the week of August 2, 2021.{{cite web |last=Pedersen |first=Erik |date=May 10, 2021 |title=HFPA Board Issues Statement on Golden Globes Controversy & Sets Timeline for Reforms: "Top Priority for Our Organization" |url=https://deadline.com/2021/05/hfpa-statement-timeline-for-reforms-1234753506/ |url-status=live |access-date=May 10, 2021 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |language=en-US |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210510213022/https://deadline.com/2021/05/hfpa-statement-timeline-for-reforms-1234753506/ |archive-date=May 10, 2021}} On October 1, 2021, the HFPA released a list of 21 new members that it had recruited under these reforms.{{cite web |last=Clayton |first=Davis |date=October 1, 2021 |title=Hollywood Foreign Press Association Adds 21 New Members With Emphasis on Diversity |url=https://variety.com/2021/awards/news/golden-globes-hfpa-2021-new-members-1235078322/ |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |access-date=October 16, 2021}} The HFPA then announced on October 15 that it still planned to hold the 79th Golden Globe Awards on January 9, 2022, with or without a telecast. It was then reported that the HFPA would not require its normal [[Golden Globe Awards#Rules|submission process and screening requirements]] for the year.{{cite web |last1=Perman |first1=Stacy |last2=Whipp |first2=Glenn |date=October 29, 2021 |title=The Golden Globes want to make a comeback this year. Hollywood isn't buying it |url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/business/story/2021-10-29/golden-globes-2022-submissions-hfpa-scandal |access-date=November 6, 2021 |work=[[Los Angeles Times]] |url-access=limited |language=en-US}} NBC ultimately aired ''[[NBC Sunday Night Football|Sunday Night Football]]'' as usual on the night of the ceremony, with the [[2021 NFL season]] having extended the regular season to 17 game, 18-week season for the first time.{{cite web |last=Simmons |first=Myles |date=January 10, 2022 |title=Sunday Night Football: After a near tie, Raiders defeat Chargers 35-32 to clinch playoff berth |url=https://profootballtalk.nbcsports.com/2022/01/10/sunday-night-football-after-a-near-tie-raiders-defeat-chargers-35-32-to-clinch-playoff-berth/ |access-date=January 10, 2022 |work=[[ProFootballTalk]] |language=en-US}} Details of the ceremony remained unclear when the HFPA announced the nominations on December 13, including whether the nominees would continue to boycott the ceremony.{{cite web |first=Conchita |last=Widjojo |date=January 5, 2022 |title=What's Going on With the 2022 Golden Globes? |url=https://wwd.com/fashion-news/fashion-scoops/2022-golden-globes-details-date-1235017183/ |access-date=December 13, 2021 |work=[[Women's Wear Daily|WWD]] |language=en-US}} Following the announcement of nominations, ''[[The New York Times]]'' wrote that the HFPA's choices represented improvement on its goal to diversify itself, but it along with many other publications doubted its effectiveness.{{cite web |last=Buchanan |first=Kyle |date=December 13, 2021 |title=Golden Globes Nominations 2022: The Complete List |language=en-US |work=[[The New York Times]] |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2021/12/13/movies/golden-globes-nominees-list.html |access-date=December 21, 2021 |url-access=limited |issn=0362-4331}}{{cite web |first1=Sonia |last1=Rao |first2=Helena |last2=Andrews-Dyer |title=Golden Globe nominations 2022: The award show everyone thought was gone is back with new nods |language=en-US |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/arts-entertainment/2021/12/13/golden-globe-nominations-2022/ |date=December 13, 2021 |access-date=December 21, 2021 |url-access=limited |issn=0190-8286}}{{cite web |last=Rich |first=Katey |title=Golden Globe Nominations 2022: See the Full List of Nominees |url=https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2021/12/golden-globe-nominations-2022-list |access-date=December 21, 2021 |work=[[Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair]] |date=December 13, 2021 |url-access=limited |language=en-US}} In support of the boycott, many studios chose not to acknowledge Golden Globes nominations in their "[[For Your Consideration (advertising)|For Your Consideration]]" marketing.{{cite web |last1=D'Alessandro |first1=Anthony |last2=Patten |first2=Dominic |date=January 6, 2022 |title=Golden Globes Will Not Be Livestreamed This Weekend |url=https://deadline.com/2022/01/golden-globes-no-livestream-nbc-hfpa-1234905661/ |url-status=live |access-date=January 10, 2022 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |language=en-US |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220106224134/https://deadline.com/2022/01/golden-globes-no-livestream-nbc-hfpa-1234905661/ |archive-date=January 6, 2022}}{{cite web |last1=Jones |first1=Nate |last2=Zhan |first2=Jennifer |date=December 15, 2021 |title=Which Golden Globe Nominees Acknowledged Their Existence? |url=https://www.vulture.com/2021/12/golden-globe-2022-nominees-response.html |access-date=January 10, 2022 |work=[[Vulture (website)|Vulture]] |url-access=limited |language=en-US}} On January 6, the HFPA announced that the ceremony would be held privately, with the winners announced via its [[social media]] platforms and [[press release]]s.{{cite web |last1=Feinberg |first1=Scott |last2=Gardner |first2=Chris |date=January 6, 2022 |title=Golden Globes to Be Held as "Private Event" With No Live Stream Available |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/golden-globes-to-be-held-as-private-event-with-no-livestream-available-1235071100/ |access-date=January 6, 2022 |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]]}} It marked the first time since the [[36th Golden Globe Awards]] in 1979 that there was no telecast and the [[65th Golden Globe Awards]] in 2008 that there was no traditional ceremony. The HFPA stated that the 90-minute event would primarily highlight the organization's philanthropic efforts and be interspersed with the award presentations. After reportedly being unable to secure celebrities to serve as presenters, attendance was limited to those associated with the HFPA's beneficiaries, and no nominees, guests, or credentialed media were in attendance.{{cite web |last=Malkin |first=Marc |date=January 4, 2022 |title=Golden Globes 2022: HFPA Fails to Secure Celebrity Presenters (EXCLUSIVE) |url=https://variety.com/2022/film/columns/golden-globes-no-celebrity-presenters-1235146563/ |url-status=live |access-date=January 5, 2022 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |language=en-US |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220104202110/https://variety.com/2022/film/columns/golden-globes-no-celebrity-presenters-1235146563/ |archive-date=January 4, 2022}} The private ceremony was held under strict [[COVID-19]] protocol due to the [[SARS-CoV-2 Omicron variant|Omicron variant]]'s widespread surge in [[Los Angeles]].{{cite web |last1=Patten |first1=Dominic |last2=Pedersen |first2=Erik |date=January 4, 2022 |title=HFPA Gives Update on Sunday's Golden Globes Ceremony: Still on Despite Covid Surge; No Celebrities; Livestream in Flux |url=https://deadline.com/2022/01/golden-globes-2022-ceremony-covid-no-celebrities-livestream-in-flux-1234904352/ |url-status=live |access-date=January 5, 2022 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |language=en-US |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220104210259/https://deadline.com/2022/01/golden-globes-2022-ceremony-covid-no-celebrities-livestream-in-flux-1234904352/ |archive-date=January 4, 2022}}{{cite magazine |last=Lenker |first=Maureen Lee |title=Golden Globes unveil plans for scaled-down 2022 event: No stars, press, or red carpet |url=https://ew.com/awards/golden-globes/golden-globes-2022-plans-no-stars-press-red-carpet/ |date=January 5, 2022 |access-date=January 5, 2022 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |language=en-US}} Supporting Actress in a Series, Miniseries, or Television Film winner]] For her role as Blanca Evangelista in ''[[Pose (TV series)|Pose]]'', [[Michaela Jaé Rodriguez]] became the first [[transgender]] person to win a Golden Globe Award, winning [[Golden Globe Award for Best Actress – Television Series Drama|Best Actress – Television Series Drama]].{{cite web |last=Hanson |first=Kait |date=January 9, 2022 |title=MJ Rodriguez becomes 1st trans actress to win a Golden Globe |url=https://www.today.com/popculture/mj-rodriguez-becomes-1st-trans-actress-win-golden-globe-rcna11559 |work=[[Today (American TV program)|Today]] |access-date=January 10, 2021}} ''West Side Story'' star [[Rachel Zegler]] was the first actress of [[Race and ethnicity in Colombia|Colombian descent]] to win [[Golden Globe Award for Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy|Best Actress – Motion Picture Musical or Comedy]] and, at 20 years old, the youngest in the category. Zegler was also one of the first people born in the 21st century to win a Golden Globe, alongside [[Billie Eilish]] (also 20 years old), who won [[Golden Globe Award for Best Original Song|Best Original Song]] for [[No Time to Die (song)|the title song]] from ''[[No Time to Die]]''.{{cite web |url=https://www.disneyfanatic.com/snow-white-actress-rachel-zegler-wins-the-golden-globe-sc1/ |title=Snow White Actress Rachel Zegler Wins the Golden Globe |date=January 10, 2022 |publisher=DisneyFanatic |access-date=January 10, 2022}} For his role as [[List of Squid Game characters#Oh Il-nam|Oh Il-nam]] in ''[[Squid Game]]'', [[O Yeong-su]] became the first [[South Korea]]n actor to win an acting award, winnnig [[Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actor – Series, Miniseries or Television Film|Best Supporting Actor – Series, Miniseries or Television Film]].{{cite web |last=Wynne |first=Kelly |date=January 10, 2022 |title=''Squid Game''{{'}}s O Yeong-su Makes History as First Korean Actor to Win a Golden Globe |url=https://people.com/tv/squid-games-o-yeong-su-becomes-first-korean-actor-to-win-golden-globe/ |work=[[People (magazine)|People]] |access-date=January 10, 2022}} ===Film=== {| class=wikitable style="width=100%" |- ! colspan=2 | Best Motion Picture |- !style="width=50%" | [[Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Drama|Drama]] !style="width=50%" | [[Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy|Musical or Comedy]] |- | valign="top" | * '''''[[The Power of the Dog (film)|The Power of the Dog]]''''' ** ''[[Belfast (film)|Belfast]]'' ** ''[[CODA (2021 film)|CODA]]'' ** ''[[Dune (2021 film)|Dune]]'' ** ''[[King Richard (film)|King Richard]]'' | valign="top" | * '''''[[West Side Story (2021 film)|West Side Story]]''''' ** ''[[Cyrano (film)|Cyrano]]'' ** ''[[Don't Look Up]]'' ** ''[[Licorice Pizza]]'' ** ''[[Tick, Tick... [[Golden Globe Award for Best Actor – Television Series Drama|Actor]] !
